economic_notes: Nation holds 100% interest in Nakoda Developments Corp and Nakota Junction Convenience Store Inc. (GBEs). Holds 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings LP and FHQTC Developments LP. Member of File Hills Qu'Appelle Tribal Council (11 nations). Portfolio investments of $12.5M held in money market, equity, and mutual funds.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED due to insufficient audit evidence for opening balances and GBE investments. Prior year (2021) auditor issued a disclaimer of opinion. Consolidated entity includes CMHC Housing Fund as fully consolidated subsidiary; Nakoda Developments Corp and Nakota Junction Convenience Store Inc. accounted for under modified equity method as GBEs; Keseechiwan Holdings LP and FHQTC Developments LP as limited partnership interests. Large CIBC Legacy Trust Loan of $6.03M taken out during year. Trust funds held by Federal Government decreased significantly from $13.2M to $575K due to $14.6M in disbursements. Land Claims expenses of $2.93M are notably high vs budget of $170K. expenses_housing set to 0 as housing is embedded in CMHC fund consolidation and not broken out as separate segment. annual_debt_service calculated as repayment of long term debt $256,735 plus repayment of capital lease obligations $59,815 = $316,550. total_government_transfers includes ISC $23,316,238 + Ottawa Trust Fund Revenue $1,940,434 + First Nations Trust $481,986 + File Hills Qu'Appelle Tribal Council $434,800 + CMHC Subsidy $315,684 + Living Sky Community Development Corp $9,833 = $25,498,975; Recognition of Unexpended Funding $1,426,156 excluded from government transfers as it is a deferral adjustment; osr calculated as total revenue $31,311,171 minus total government transfers $25,558,375 = $5,752,796 (includes Trans Canada Pipeline, Other Revenue, Rental Income, GBE earnings, LP earnings, Recognition of Unexpended Funding).
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
